creative_brief: Clean, confident layout — not startup-techy, not rustic-cute. Hero image: a real person collecting a box of produce at the Brookvale hub, natural light, no staged smiles. Muted greens and warm neutrals. Below the fold, a simple diagram showing the short supply chain (farm → hub → you) next to the long one. Investment section uses a slightly different background tone to signal the shift from story to action.
headline: You already buy the food. What if you owned the store?
subhead: FarmThru is a farm-direct grocery store cutting out unnecessary middlemen — and we're letting everyday customers become owners through equity crowdfunding. Starting at just $50.
hero_copy:
Every week, you hand money to a system that charges more and delivers less. Prices keep climbing. The farmers growing your food barely break even. And somewhere in between, middlemen take their cut without adding value you can taste.

FarmThru is a different model — a hub-and-collect grocery store sourcing direct from partner farms across NSW. No unnecessary middlemen. No lock-in subscriptions. Just real food, priced fairly.

Now we're offering something the big chains never would: a seat at the table.

sections.heading: The investment thesis in plain language
sections.body:
Supermarkets have trained us to accept a broken supply chain as normal. Food travels thousands of kilometres. Farmers get squeezed. Customers pay the markup.

FarmThru's model removes the unnecessary steps between farm and family. We partner with ~15–20 farms and suppliers across NSW. Customers order online and collect from our Brookvale hub, Monday to Friday. No warehouse empire. No fleet of delivery trucks. Lower overheads, better margins, fresher food.

Our bulk buying model rewards commitment — customers save by buying in larger quantities, which means higher average orders and stronger unit economics. It's a grocery store built around efficiency, not excess.

This isn't charity. It's a business designed to compete on price, quality, and trust — three things the big chains are quietly losing.

sections.heading: What your $50 actually builds
sections.body:
Through equity crowdfunding on Birchal, you can own a real piece of FarmThru — starting at $50, up to a maximum of $10,000 per investor.

Your investment helps scale a model that already works at hub level: farm-direct sourcing, on-demand ordering with no membership fees, and a supply chain short enough that you could meet the people growing your food.

This isn't a donation or a pre-order. It's ownership. You share in the upside if we grow — and you carry the risk if we don't. That honesty matters to us.

Want early access before the campaign goes live? A $5 refundable deposit gets you VIP priority — first access to invest, exclusive Q&A sessions, and updates before anyone else sees them.

sections.heading: Why now, and why this matters
sections.body:
The gap between what Australians want and what supermarkets offer is widening every quarter. People want to know where their food comes from. They want to support regenerative farming. They want fair prices without the guilt.

FarmThru was built for that gap. Not as a premium luxury brand — as an everyday grocery store. Hub-and-collect keeps our costs low. Farm-direct keeps the food honest. And equity crowdfunding means the people who eat the food can be the same people who own the company.

No commitment beyond what you choose. No lock-in. Order when you want. Collect from Brookvale Monday to Friday. Be part of building something the big chains can't replicate — because they'd have to dismantle themselves to try.

sections.heading: Compliance
sections.body: FarmThru's equity crowdfunding campaign is hosted on Birchal. Investing in crowdfunded opportunities involves risks, including the potential loss of your entire investment. This is not financial advice. Past performance is not a reliable indicator of future results. No returns are guaranteed. You should read the offer document and consider whether this investment is appropriate for your circumstances before making any decision. Investment caps apply: minimum $50, maximum $10,000 per investor.
